title: Snow removal equipment
abstract: A snow removal equipment (200) is described. The snow removal equipment (200) comprises a propulsion means allowing the snow removal equipment to move on a terrain, a gathering unit (210) adapted for gathering or shovelling snow on the terrain, and a disposal unit (220) to remove the gathered snow. The snow removal equipment (200) has been adapted to allow the speed at which the snow on the terrain is gathered, and the speed at which the gathered snow is removed, to be controlled separately.
claims:
Claims 1. - A snow removal equipment (200) comprising - means of propulsion allowing the snow removal equipment (230) to move on a terrain, - a gathering unit (210) adapted for gathering or shovelling snow on the terrain, - a disposal unit (220) for removing the gathered snow, the snow removal equipment (200) being adapted to allow the speed at which the snow on the terrain is gathered, and the speed at which the gathered snow is removed, to be controlled separately. 2. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to claim 1, the snow removal equipment (200) being an unmanned snow removal equipment (200). 3. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the speed at which the snow on the terrain is gathered, and the speed at which the gathered snow is removed, can be controlled independently. 4. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of the preceding claims, the snow removal equipment (200) additionally comprising a chamber, wherein the gathering unit (210) is adapted for transporting the gathered snow to a chamber (215), and wherein the disposal unit (220) is connected to the chamber and adapted to remove snow from the chamber (215). 5. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of the preceding claims, the disposal unit (220) being adapted to blow off the snow. 6. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of the preceding claims, the snow removal equipment (200) comprising a controller for coordinating the speed at which the snow on the terrain is gathered, with the speed at which the gathered snow is removed. 7. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of the preceding claims, the snow removal equipment (200) comprising a motor (Ml) for driving the gathering unit (210) and a motor (M2) for driving the disposal unit (220), the motor (Ml) for driving the gathering unit (210) and the motor (M2) for driving the disposal unit (220) being separately drivable. 8. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of the preceding claims, to the extent dependent on claim 6, wherein the controller (250) is adapted to individually control the speed at which the (210) gathering unit is driven and the speed at which the disposal unit (220) is driven. 9. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to claim 8, wherein the controller (250) is programmed to reduce the speed at which the gathering unit (210) is driven if the snow cannot be removed sufficiently quickly. 10. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to claim 8, wherein the controller (250) is programmed to increase the speed at which the disposal unit (220) is driven if the snow cannot be removed sufficiently quickly. 11. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of the preceding claims to the extent dependent on claim 6, wherein the controller (250) is adapted to slow down the snow- gathering engine (M2) with respect to the snow-removal engine (M2) and/or accelerate the snow-removal engine (M2) relative to the snow-gathering engine (M2) if the amount of snow gathered or shovelled by the gathering unit is greater than the amount of snow removed by the disposal unit (220). 12. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein a motor for driving the gathering unit (210) can also be used for driving the propulsion means (230). 13. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of claims 1 to 11, the snow removal equipment (200) comprising one or more separate motors (M3) for driving the propulsion means (230). 14. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of the preceding claims, to the extent dependent on claim 6, the controller (250) being adapted to individually control the speed at which the propulsion means (230) are driven and the speed at which the disposal unit (220) is driven. 15. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to claim 14, wherein the controller (250) is programmed to reduce the speed at which the propulsion means (230) are driven if the snow cannot be removed sufficiently quickly. 16. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to claim 15, the controller (250) being programmed to set at least one intermediate speed between 0 km/h and the maximum speed for driving the propulsion means (230) or for reversing the drive direction of the propulsion means (230) so as to provide this movement in the opposite direction. 17.- A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of the preceding claims, the snow removal equipment (200) comprising a sensor (260) or camera for detecting the accumulation of snow or ice formation. 18. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to claim 17, the sensor being an optical sensor located in the chamber (215) or the snow removal equipment comprising a laser. 19. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to claim 17, the sensor being a weight or pressure sensor. 20.- A snow removal equipment (200) according to claim 17, the sensor being a sensor for measuring the power output of the at least one engine for advancing the snow removal equipment (200), a temperature sensor, or an ultrasonic sensor. 21. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of claims 17 to 20, the controller being programmed to control the different motors based on an input signal from the at least one sensor. 22. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to claim 21, to the extent dependent on claim 4, wherein the input signal indicates when the amount of snow in the chamber exceeds a predetermined value, when the fill ratio of the chamber is greater than a predetermined value, when the amount or flow rate or average flow rate of snow entering the chamber via the gathering unit (210) is greater than the amount or flow rate of snow leaving the chamber via the disposal unit (220), or when ice formation is detected. 23. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of the preceding claims, the snow disposal unit (220) being based on an Archimedean screw. 24. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of the preceding claims, the snow disposal unit (220) being based on a blowing or screw pump system. 25. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of the preceding claims, the snow removal equipment additionally comprising a brush (410) for sweeping the surface, after snow has been gathered by means of the gathering unit (210). 26. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to claim 25, wherein the brush (410) is a flat brush mounted centrally. 27. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of the preceding claims, the snow removal equipment (200) having at least one battery for supplying electric power. 28. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to claim 1, the snow removal equipment (200) including a controller for the automated removing of snow, without the operator having to control the snow removal equipment (200). 29. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of the preceding claims, the snow removal equipment (200) being provided with a processor, a memory module, and a remote control for control of the snow removal equipment by an operator, the processor 

 being programmed to perform a calibration procedure upon first putting the snow removal equipment into service or when invoking a calibration period, the calibration procedure comprising the controlled travelling of a route and storing the route in the memory module for later use as a pre-programmed route during automated operation without operator. 30. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of the preceding claims, the snow removal equipment (200) being provided with a handle for controlling the snow removal equipment (200), for example, during calibration. 31. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of the preceding claims, further comprising a wireless receiver for receiving signals from a corresponding remote control, the controller being connected to the wireless receiver and provided for moving the snow removal equipment based on the received commands. 32. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of the preceding claims, the snow removal equipment (200) being provided with detection means for detecting a boundary of the terrain or a position on the terrain. 33. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to the preceding claim, the detection means comprising a camera and/or an optical sensor and/or a laser and/or a temperature sensor and/or an ultrasonic sensor, the processor being programmed with an image-processing algorithm provided with a module for object recognition. 34.- A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of the preceding claims, the snow removal equipment (200) being provided with a self-learning function for detecting a boundary of the terrain or a position on the terrain. 35. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of the preceding claims, the snow removal equipment (200) being provided with a programmable module, it being possible to program the programmable module directly or remotely with the boundaries of the zone to be removed. 36. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to any of the preceding claims, the snow removal equipment (200) being adapted for unmounting the gathering unit and mounting a lawn mower unit or a leaf blower or leaf vacuum unit, or a brush unit, or an aerating unit, or a water spraying unit. 37. - A snow removal equipment (200) according to claim 36, the snow removal equipment (200) being adapted with a quick coupling system for rapidly exchanging the 

 gathering unit with a lawn mower unit or leaf blower or leaf vacuum unit, or a brush unit, or a aerating unit, or a water spraying unit. 38. - A method for removing snow using the snow removal equipment (200) according to any of claims 1 to 37, comprising the following steps: - driving an gathering unit (210) adapted for gathering or shovelling snow on the terrain, - the removal of the gathered snow from the snow removal equipment (200), wherein the speed at which the gathering unit (210) is driven and the speed at which the gathered snow is removed can be controlled separately. 39. - A method according to claim 38, wherein the speed at which the gathering unit (200) is driven and the speed at which the gathered snow is removed can be controlled independently. 40. - A method for removing snow according to claim 38, in which the method further comprises the automated driving of the snow removal equipment, without the intervention of an operator. 41.- A method for removing snow according to any of claims 38 to 40, the method comprising: performing a calibration procedure upon first putting the snow removal equipment (200) into service or when invoking a calibration period, the calibration procedure comprising the controlled travelling of a route and/or comprising a self-learning process for learning a route and the calibration procedure comprising storing the route in the memory module for later use as a pre-programmed route during automated operation without operator. 42.- A computer program product which, when executed on a processor, performs the steps according to the snow removing method as provided in any of claims 38 to 41.
